description: All-day event gets specific time - should prefer specific time over vague all-day

actual.title: Annual Company Picnic
actual.description: Annual summer picnic for all employees and families. Annual summer picnic starts at 11am. Bring your family! BBQ lunch served at noon.

content_parts.text:
You are merging calendar event data from multiple emails.

**Existing Event:**
- Title: Annual Company Picnic
- Start: 2026-07-20T00:00:00
- End: 2026-07-20T23:59:59
- All Day: True
- Location: City Park
- Description: Annual summer picnic for all employees and families
- Importance: action_required

**New Information (source type: update):**
- Title: Annual Company Picnic
- Start: 2026-07-20T11:00:00
- End: 2026-07-20T15:00:00
- All Day: False
- Location: City Park - Pavilion 3
- Description: Annual summer picnic starts at 11am. Bring your family! BBQ lunch served at noon.
- Importance: action_required

**Merge Rules:**
1. If source_type is "cancellation", prefix title with "CANCELLED: " (if not already)
2. Prefer specific time (e.g., 6-7pm) over all-day
3. Combine descriptions, keeping all relevant info (append new info)
4. Use most specific location (longer address usually better)
5. Keep newer times if they differ (updates)
6. For importance: keep the highest level (action_required > fyi)

Output JSON with merged event data:
{
    "title": "...",
    "start_datetime": "...",
    "end_datetime": "...",
    "all_day": true/false,
    "location": "...",
    "description": "...",
    "importance": "action_required" or "fyi"
}

Annual summer picnic starts at 11am. Bring your family! BBQ lunch served at noon.","importance":"action_required"}
